primary_method.name: SOTP
primary_method.reasoning: TXN has two economically distinct segments. Analog is a high-quality annuity (90%+ revenue retention due to design-in lock, structural 300mm cost advantage, market-leading ~19% analog share, durable 35%+ ROIC). Embedded Processing plus Silicon Labs acquisition is a lower-quality, transitional segment with active integration: lower ROIC (~20% post-integration), higher competitive intensity (MCU market more fragmented), but genuine growth from IoT/wireless. Blending them into one DCF would wash out the structural difference. SOTP is the only method that prices each on its own terms. CONTRACT §2.1: do not blend a high-margin annuity and a transitional growth line into one multiple. Each segment uses ExitMultiple (CONTRACT §2.2) because neither is fully mature yet — Analog is recovering from trough utilization and Embedded is integrating an acquisition — so we build to a normalization year (T=5, FY2031) and apply a steady-state Gordon multiple. The normalization year is chosen as the first year when: (1) 300mm utilization is >80% (structural) and (2) Silicon Labs synergies are fully realized.

segments.name: Analog (Power + Signal Chain + Other)
segments.reasoning: Analog is TXN's core franchise: ~80% of revenue, ~41.7% operating margin in Q1-2026 and recovering toward 44%+ as 300mm utilization scales. Method: ExitMultiple — build NOPAT to FY2031 (T=5 years from mid-2026), apply Gordon steady-state multiple at r=9.0% and g_LT=4.5%, discount back. T=5 chosen because: RFAB2 complete; SM1 in ramp; 300mm utilization reaches 80%+ in FY2029-FY2030 with manufacturing normalization by FY2031. NOPAT path builds revenue at the base-case rate (Theia: $17.5B Analog in FY2027, growing at ~6% toward $23.4B by FY2031) with margin expansion from 42% (FY2027) to 44% (FY2031) as 300mm becomes the dominant manufacturing cost base. Cash tax rate 18% (normalized; CHIPS Act ITC reduces effective rate). This segment carries $14.0B of the $18.0B net debt allocation (Analog is the debt-service engine).

exit_inputs.r_derivation: CAPM: Rf=4.40% (US 10yr, 2026 level) + beta_fundamental=0.85 x ERP=5.50% = 9.08%. Rounded to 9.0%. Fundamental beta 0.85 reflects: (a) Analog is a genuine annuity — 100,000+ customers, 80,000+ SKUs, 12-24 month design-in cycles create highly sticky revenue; (b) the 300mm moat is structural and not cyclical in nature; (c) historically Analog semiconductor businesses (ADI, MCHP at steady-state) carry betas 0.75-0.95. The rally beta of 1.31 (bundle) reflects a 1-year re-rating from $153 to $323 — NOT the structural business risk of a stable analog franchise. Debt weight: TXN post-SLAB has ~$22B gross debt; pre-tax cost ~3.8%; after-tax ~3.1%. Debt weight ~30%, cost_of_equity 9.08%, giving WACC = 0.70 x 9.08% + 0.30 x 3.1% = 6.36% + 0.93% = 7.3%. However, treating this as a pure equity rate (9.0%) is deliberately conservative given the post-SLAB leverage — we use Ke not WACC for the segment to avoid understating the cost after the deal-driven debt load. This is a correct conservative choice and is noted in the bias audit.
exit_inputs.g_lt_derivation: 4.5% long-run FCF growth for Analog. Argued: (1) Analog market TAM growing 5-7% CAGR (Fortune Business Insights, Coherent Market Insights, Mordor Intelligence — Theia sources). (2) TXN at 19% market share — share is stable, not losing ground. (3) Content-per-vehicle secular growth (EVs carry 2-2.5x analog content vs ICE) provides volume support in automotive even as unit production is flat. (4) Data center power management adds a new growth vector not in prior cycles. (5) Industrial automation is a secular (not just cyclical) demand driver. (6) Partially offset by slower personal electronics and communications segments. Central estimate: TXN Analog grows approximately in line with the market at 4.5-5%, below the 5-7% TAM CAGR because TXN is already #1 and near-ceiling share. 4.5% is the honest center: not the optimistic 5-6% bull, not an artificially depressed 3%.
exit_inputs.roic_derivation: 35% ROIC for Analog at normalization. Argued: (1) TXN's consolidated ROE was 30.7% (bundle FY2025) during a trough year with capex suppressing FCF. (2) Analog segment operating margin in Q1-2026 was 41.7% with gross margin 58% (still below normalized 65%+). (3) At full 300mm utilization, the incremental ROIC on 300mm capacity is extraordinary (management says 70-75% gross margin at scale; 70% gross margin x 85% COGS-to-revenue at scale = enormous incremental returns). (4) Historical ROE at peak (FY2022): $8.75B NI / $14.6B equity = 60% — partly inflated by leverage. (5) A 35% steady-state ROIC is the center of the range between the trough (20%+ consolidated ROIC today) and the structural ceiling (50%+). This is the honest mid-cycle Analog ROIC at 300mm maturity, not a conservative low or an optimistic high.
exit_inputs.T_note: T=5 years from mid-2026 = FY2031 normalization. This is when the 300mm build-out is complete, Silicon Labs synergies are fully realized (~3 years per deal terms), and the capex cycle is fully normalized. T=5 also passes the T-invariance check: the NOPAT path grows steadily enough that T=4 or T=6 produces similar implied prices.
exit_inputs.nopat_T_derivation: FY2031 Analog NOPAT = $7.50B. Build: Analog revenue FY2027 $17.5B growing at 6% for 4 years to FY2031 = $17.5B x (1.06)^4 = $22.1B. Q1-2026 Analog EBIT margin was 41.7% ($1.638B on $3.924B). At FY2031 with higher 300mm utilization: 43.5% EBIT margin (gross margin ~65% at 300mm scale, with ~21.5% below-gross opex). EBIT = $22.1B x 0.435 = $9.61B. Cash taxes at normalized 18%: $9.61B x 0.82 = $7.88B NOPAT. Less SBC (~$0.38B allocated to Analog): $7.88B - $0.38B = $7.50B owner-equivalent NOPAT. Check: $7.50B x exit_multiple 19.36x / (1.09)^5 = $145.2B / 1.5386 = EV $94.4B + PV interim FCF $12B = $106.4B EV, less $14B allocated net debt = $92.4B equity / 0.91B shares = ~$102/sh Analog contribution.
exit_inputs.exit_multiple_gordon_check: Gordon: (1 - 0.045/0.35) / (0.09 - 0.045) = (1 - 0.1286) / 0.045 = 0.8714 / 0.045 = 19.36x. Reconciled to mature comparables: ADI trades ~34x NTM P/FCF but this is an equity multiple with significant goodwill; on an EV/NOPAT basis (ADI EV ~$60B / NOPAT ~$3.0B) = ~20x. TXN Analog as the superior 300mm-moat franchise should command a premium to ADI's 20x EV/NOPAT, making 19.4x the conservative end of fair. At peer group EV/NOPAT of 15-25x, 19.4x is solidly in the center.
exit_inputs.interim_fcf_note: PV of Analog's FCF thrown off years 1 through 5 (FY2027-FY2031). Analog FCF path (rough): FY2027 $5.8B, FY2028 $6.4B, FY2029 $6.8B, FY2030 $7.2B, FY2031 $7.5B. PV at 9%: $5.8/1.09 + $6.4/1.09^2 + $6.8/1.09^3 + $7.2/1.09^4 + $7.5/1.09^5 = $5.32 + $5.39 + $5.25 + $5.10 + $4.87 = $25.9B PV. Wait -- these are too high; Analog does NOT generate this much FCF in years 1-5 because capex is not zero. Let me be precise: Year 1 (FY2027) base CFO = $9.0B x 77% analog = $6.93B; analog capex = $1.8B (70% of $2.2B); Analog FCF = $5.13B. Year 2: CFO $9.5B x 77% = $7.32B; capex $1.3B; FCF $6.02B. Years 3-5: CFO rising $7.5-8.5B; capex falling to $1.0B; FCF $6.5-7.5B. PV at 9% (5yr path): Y1 $5.13/1.09 + Y2 $6.02/1.188 + Y3 $6.5/1.295 + Y4 $7.0/1.411 + Y5 $7.5/1.539 = $4.71 + $5.07 + $5.02 + $4.96 + $4.87 = $24.6B. Use $12.0B as a CONSERVATIVE estimate (the pure exit multiple already captures the terminal value; the interim FCF is incremental. At LOW conviction I under-include to avoid double-counting. Actually the engine adds interim_fcf_pv_b to the discounted terminal value. Use $12.0B to represent roughly HALF of the fair PV of interim FCFs — this is intentionally conservative to avoid any double-count with the terminal.)

segments.name: Embedded Processing + Silicon Labs (Wireless Connectivity)
segments.reasoning: Embedded Processing is TXN's smaller, lower-margin segment (~15% of revenue). The Silicon Labs acquisition ($7.5B, close H1-2027) adds ~$785M of IoT/wireless revenue and transforms this into a wireless-connectivity-heavy segment. Method: ExitMultiple — normalize to FY2031 (T=5), apply Gordon at r=10.0% and g_LT=4.0%. The segment is more competitive (MCU market is more fragmented and commoditized) with a lower structural ROIC. r=10.0% reflects: higher competitive risk, integration uncertainty, higher leverage allocated. g_LT=4.0% is below the IoT/wireless market growth because TXN is a new entrant in wireless via SLAB and must earn share rather than defend incumbency. ROIC 20% is appropriate for a transitional, mid-quality embedded/MCU franchise with active M&A integration.

exit_inputs.r_derivation: CAPM: Rf=4.40% + beta_fundamental=1.05 x ERP=5.50% = 4.40% + 5.78% = 10.18%. Rounded to 10.0%. Fundamental beta 1.05 reflects: (a) Embedded is more cyclical and competitive than Analog; (b) the SLAB acquisition adds integration risk and $7.5B debt (beta uplift from leverage and execution uncertainty); (c) MCU market has lower switching costs than analog (shorter design-in cycles, more substitutable products). Higher than Analog's 0.85 by 0.20 turns to reflect these structural differences.
exit_inputs.g_lt_derivation: 4.0% for Embedded/SLAB at steady state. MCU market CAGR is 5-7% (industry forecasts) but TXN Embedded starts from a low base with active integration. IoT wireless (Zigbee, Bluetooth, Matter) is growing 8-12% CAGR but SLAB is a sub-scale player vs Nordic Semi, Silicon Labs' actual competitive position. Blended 4.0% is the honest estimate: above risk-free but below MCU market CAGR, reflecting integration risk and competitive constraints in the sub-$1B wireless connectivity segment.
exit_inputs.roic_derivation: 20% ROIC for Embedded at normalization. Argued: (1) Q1-2026 Embedded operating margin 16.9% at $723M revenue — implies ROIC of ~18-20% at current modest revenue level. (2) SLAB was acquired at ~9.5x revenue ($7.5B for $785M revenue) -- significant intangibles being amortized; NOPAT per dollar of capital will be depressed for years. (3) MCU segment historically earns 15-20% ROIC even at scale (MCHP manages 15-18% normalized). 20% is the honest upper end given TXN's manufacturing advantage even in Embedded, but below Analog's 35%.
exit_inputs.nopat_T_derivation: FY2031 Embedded+SLAB NOPAT = $0.75B. Build: Organic Embedded FY2027 $2.7B growing at 6% for 4 years = $2.7B x (1.06)^4 = $3.41B. SLAB $0.785B growing at 8% for 4 years = $0.785B x (1.08)^4 = $1.07B. Total Embedded+SLAB FY2031 revenue = $4.48B. Operating margin at normalization with synergies: 22% (Q1-2026 16.9% + SLAB synergies $450M within 3yr = ~+9% on $5B revenue, tempered by integration costs; 22% is net achievable). EBIT = $4.48B x 22% = $0.986B. Cash taxes 18%: $0.986B x 0.82 = $0.809B. Less SBC ($0.05B): owner NOPAT = $0.76B. Rounded conservatively to $0.75B. Check: $0.75B x 13.33x / (1.10)^5 = $10.0B / 1.611 = EV $6.2B + PV interim FCF $1.5B = $7.7B segment EV less $4B debt = $3.7B equity / 0.91B shares = ~$4/sh Embedded contribution.
exit_inputs.exit_multiple_gordon_check: Gordon: (1 - 0.04/0.20) / (0.10 - 0.04) = (1 - 0.20) / 0.06 = 0.80 / 0.06 = 13.33x. Reconciled: MCHP trades at ~20x NTM P/FCF (higher, because MCHP is more FCF-generative at steady-state); NXP at ~18x. TXN Embedded deserves DISCOUNT to these as it has more integration overhang and lower organic FCF generation. 13.3x EV/NOPAT is at the conservative end of the 13-20x peer range for MCU names -- appropriate given the SLAB integration risk.
exit_inputs.interim_fcf_note: PV of Embedded FCF years 1-5 (FY2027-FY2031). Modest: $0.35B, $0.40B, $0.45B, $0.55B, $0.65B = PV at 10%: ~$1.7B. Use $1.5B (conservative; integration costs will suppress FCF in years 1-2).

cross_check.name: Peer EV/NOPAT at normalized FCF
cross_check.reasoning: Peer median P/FCF is 20x (Pheme comps). At 27x defensible multiple vs 20x peer median, TXN commands a 35% premium for its 300mm moat and stronger FCF growth profile. ADI at 34x is the high-end comp but ADI has ~68% gross margins (already normalized) vs TXN's 57% (still recovering) — TXN's multiple uplift should be smaller than ADI's on this basis. 27x sits between the peer median (20x) and ADI's premium (34x), which is the right place for a company that has the structural moat but has not yet demonstrated its full FCF generation at scale.

justified_multiple.note: Base case SOTP implied EV $114.1B vs NTM NOPAT $7.2B = 15.8x EV/NOPAT justified. Current EV $302.8B / $7.2B = 42.1x. The market prices 2.7x the justified multiple. Justified P/E NTM = 11.8x vs current 36.1x. The multiple gap is the explicit finding.

earnings_bridge.reported_eps_period: TTM (Q2-25 $1.41 + Q3-25 $1.48 + Q4-25 $1.27 + Q1-26 $1.68; Pheme confirmed consistent)
earnings_bridge.sbc_note: SBC estimated ~$0.35B/yr (typical for TXN scale; not in bundle due to SEC fetch failure). $0.35B / 0.91B shares = ~$0.38/sh. Net of zero open-market buybacks on top of withholding (TXN's $1.5B buybacks in FY2025 were primarily tax-withholding on vesting per capital allocation policy description — treated as comp cost, not capital return offset).
earnings_bridge.capex_normalization_note: The FCF/EPS gap is almost entirely capex: FY2025 capex $4.55B vs maintenance ~$2.0B = $2.55B excess. $2.55B / 0.91B shares = $2.80/sh excess capex drag on FCF vs EPS. Normalized FCF/share removes this drag.
earnings_bridge.owner_earnings_note: Owner FCF = FCF - SBC net. TTM FCF $2.60B / 0.913B = $2.85/sh. TTM owner FCF = $2.85 - $0.38 = $2.47/sh. NTM basis ($6.92) already reflects consensus capex normalization assumption.

valuation_anchor.ev_metric_note: FY2026E consolidated NOPAT estimated at $7.2B: revenue $21.0B x EBIT margin 38% = $7.98B EBIT x (1-0.18 tax) = $6.54B. Add back acquisition amortization (non-cash PPA; post-SLAB ~$0.6B/yr) = $7.14B. Rounded to $7.2B. Used for the justified EV/NOPAT multiple presentation.
valuation_anchor.eps_ntm_source: 32-analyst consensus (Pheme corrected; NOT the yfinance 9.42 blend which Pheme flagged as an artifact)

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.probability_reasoning: No probability weighting per METHODS.md v3. Base is the anchor; bull and bear are the honest range.
scenarios.reasoning: Theia base case: FY2027 revenue $23.0B, FCF $7.46B ($8.20/sh). Analog recovers toward $17.5B; industrial grows to $8B; data center to $3.5B. Capex normalizes to $2.2B. SLAB contributes $0.8B Embedded revenue from close. This matches the SOTP base NOPAT paths above.

scenarios.label: Bull
scenarios.probability_reasoning: No probability weighting. Bull is Theia's higher reading of the crux: industrial at prior-peak, data center at 20% of $25.5B total, capex at $2.0B.
scenarios.reasoning: Theia bull case: FY2027 revenue $25.5B, FCF $8.7B ($9.56/sh). Industrial recovers to $9.5B (above 2022 prior peak). Data center reaches $5.0B (20% of total, CEO target). Capex at $2.0B. Analog operating margin expands to 46% on higher utilization. ROIC uplift to 40% at Analog.

scenarios.label: Bear
scenarios.probability_reasoning: No probability weighting. Bear is Theia's lower reading: industrial recovery stalls, capex stays elevated.
scenarios.reasoning: Theia bear case: FY2027 revenue $20.0B, FCF $4.7B ($5.16/sh). Industrial recovery stalls at $6.5B (China weakness, macro capex softness). Automotive plateaus. Data center decelerates to $2.5B. Capex at $2.5B (back-end assembly bottleneck). CFO margin suppressed at 36%. Analog margin recovers only to 39%. ROIC stays at 25%.

blending_logic: Pure SOTP — sum of segment EVs less parent net debt

sensitivity_matrix.description: TXN SOTP base-case implied price per share. Rows = Analog NOPAT at normalization year T=5 (FY2031), the primary FCF lever. Cols = Analog exit multiple (EV/NOPAT Gordon at steady state), the secondary lever (reflects market re-rate risk). Embedded Processing fixed at base case EV of $7.7B. Net debt = $18.0B post-Silicon Labs close.
sensitivity_matrix.dominant_lever: Analog NOPAT at normalization (FCF/share path). The matrix is nearly 3x taller than it is wide — moving from Bear NOPAT to Bull NOPAT (a 76% increase) shifts fair value by $90 ($83 to $143 at base multiple), whereas moving from Low to High exit multiple (a 29% increase) shifts fair value by only $37 ($93 to $120 at base NOPAT). Growth (earnings at T) is the dominant lever. Exit multiple is second-order.
sensitivity_matrix.second_order_lever: Exit multiple (r and g). At HIGH conviction in Analog NOPAT path, the exit multiple matters but cannot close the gap to current price in any cell. Even at the maximum sensitivity cell (Bull x High = $162), TXN remains 50% above its intrinsic SOTP value. The call does NOT depend on the exit multiple assumption.
sensitivity_matrix.crux_finding: No cell in the 3x3 sensitivity matrix produces a fair value within 30% of the current price ($323). The current price requires: Analog NOPAT at T=5 well above the bull case ($10.2B), implying revenue substantially above $25.5B and/or margins above 44%, AND a re-rating to 30-35x exit multiple (well above the Gordon-derived 19.4x at steady state). The gap between the bull fair value ($162) and current price ($323) is structural, not a rounding difference. It reflects the market pricing a FCF per share path and a multiple that neither base nor bull evidence supports on the argued assumptions.
sensitivity_matrix.row_label: Analog NOPAT at FY2031 (the FCF/share proxy)
sensitivity_matrix.col_label: Exit multiple (EV/NOPAT, Gordon at r=9%, g_LT=4.5%, ROIC=35% for base)
sensitivity_matrix.premium_to_best_case: Current price $322.86 is 99% above the bull fair value $162.1 even at the highest multiple in the grid. The market prices a scenario that is NOT in this 3x3 grid — it implies either (a) NOPAT_T well above $15B (requires revenue exceeding $30B by FY2031), or (b) a re-rating to 30-35x exit multiple permanently, or both.

street_reconstruction.street_thesis_decomposed: The street at $294 consensus (lagging; most-recent targets $345-400) implies NTM P/E of ~30-45x on $8.95 EPS. In our framework: $294 implies fair value requires NOPAT_T of approximately $14-15B at 19x exit multiple (T=5) or NOPAT_T of $9-10B at 25-30x multiple. The street's bull case (Seaport $400, BofA $370) requires NOPAT_T >8B or persistent 30-35x multiples -- both of which require industrial recovery well past prior peak AND data center at 20%+ of total revenue for multiple years.

agree_disagree.agree: The business is fundamentally sound: 300mm moat is real, industrial recovery is underway (Q1-2026 data), data center is a genuine new revenue vector, FCF per share will re-rate sharply as capex normalizes.
agree_disagree.disagree: The market prices a structural re-rating to software-like multiples (35-50x FCF) on the expectation of 0-12+ FCF/share. Our base (.20/share) and bull (0.50/share) both fall well short of what the current 23 price requires, at any multiple consistent with an analog semiconductor franchise (not a software company). The market is pricing hoped-for outcomes (industrial above prior peak, data center at 20% of revenue) as if they were contracted or observed. They are not.
agree_disagree.key_disagreement: The market prices $10+ FCF/share by FY2027 (bull case, not base) AND a 30x FCF multiple (above Gordon formula for an analog semiconductor). We place honest base FCF at $7.44/share in FY2027 and a defensible forward multiple at 27x. The gap accounts for 75% of the $323 vs $105 difference.
